title: Transparent conducting oxides for wearable optical stacks
abstract: Transparent conducting oxides such as indium oxide, tin oxide, and zinc oxide are useful in displays and sensors. Doping strategies can improve conductivity while preserving visible transparency. Scarce elements motivate alternatives to indium-rich compositions.

title: Atomic layer deposition barriers for lightweight devices
abstract: Alumina, zirconia, and silica films made by atomic layer deposition or plasma enhanced chemical vapor deposition can provide moisture barriers and scratch-resistant surfaces for compact optical hardware.

title: Transparent spinel ceramics for durable optical windows
abstract: Magnesium aluminate spinel combines optical transmission with hardness, thermal stability, and moderate density. Processing quality controls haze and scattering losses.

title: Lead-free piezoelectric perovskites for compact actuators
abstract: Barium titanate and strontium titanate families offer lead-free oxide platforms for sensing, dielectric tuning, and piezoelectric actuation. Domain engineering and dopants can improve electromechanical coupling.

title: PZT as a high-response actuator reference
abstract: Lead zirconate titanate near the morphotropic boundary remains a high-performance piezoelectric reference, but lead toxicity and process constraints motivate safer alternatives for consumer-facing hardware.

title: Electroactive polymers for soft robotic skins
abstract: PVDF-based fluoropolymers and silicone elastomers enable flexible actuators and stretchable sensing skins. They trade lower force density for low mass, high compliance, and scalable processing.

title: HPC screening loops for materials discovery
abstract: Closed-loop materials discovery can combine retrieval, candidate generation, DFT, molecular dynamics, Monte Carlo simulation, and active learning. Scheduler templates and structured outputs make computational campaigns reproducible.

title: Synthesis-aware AI screening for practical discovery
abstract: AI-generated materials candidates should be filtered by precursor availability, process complexity, toxicity, supply risk, and characterization burden. Synthesis-aware ranking improves the chance that computational hits can become real experiments.
